entry.title: Company Code Carve out or Separation in SAP ERP system (SAP ECC & S4HANA On-Prem,Cloud environments)
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog outlines the typical business triggers that drive the need for Company Code Carve out in a productive ERP environment (ECC or S4HANA systems). It also highlights the possible realization methodologies to support such business transformations effectively.

Company Code Carve Out

A Company Code Carve-Out refers to the selective separation of data from a productive SAP instance into a separate target system. This data separation is driven by the organizational unit “Company Code” (technical domain: BUKRS) within the SAP system.

As part of the process, both higher-level organizational elements (e.g., Controlling Area, Operating Concern etc.) and lower-level hierarchical elements (e.g., Plant, Sales Organization, etc.) within the SAP enterprise structure are identified and analyzed to enable the separation of the entire legal entity’s business application data.

The dataset in scope typically includes all relevant data stored in SAP database tables and fields across all layers Viz. Customizing, Master, and Transactional data. This also encompasses customer-specific developments stored in Z or Y namespace tables.

Goal of Company Code Carve out is to give the divested business entity into an independent environment while minimizing the impact on the source system that contains the remaining data related to the relevant company codes.

Typical business use cases that require Company Code Carve out 

  1. Divesture of a Business Unit: It happens when the organization or group decides to sell off/dispose or close a part of the organization or subsidiary in order to raise cash/address regulatory or performance issues. With this activity, divested group can either become a stand-alone entity or independent company or strategic asset for a buyer.

  2. Country or Region-specific Separations/Decentralization of Central System: When an organization decides to spin-off or separates the country or region from their existing ECC or S4HANA landscape. These situations are primarily driven by the differences in the corporate laws, securities, regulations, tax rules and controls related to that country jurisdiction. Sometimes, this may also be driven due to the sanctions amended on specific countries leading to damage to the business in whole.

  3. Joint Venture: When organizations or partners decide to enter into JV with specific scope & duration to access country specific local markets, shared investments, risk diversifications and later decides to divest or spin off into a separate legal entity.

  4. Sale of Subsidiaries: An event occurs when parent organization plans to sale/transfer controls of a legal entity to another organization based on the board approvals, share holders’ resolution etc., for additional liquidity or strategic alignment on existing business.

  5. Strategic Refocus and Legal Restructuring: This kind of activity defines the business and reshapes the legal entity structure to support the strategy that organization thinks to refocusing their business. It helps in reallocation of capital to high advantage business or withdraw from misaligned businesses or non-core portfolio.

  6. Merger & Acquisitions: Mergers & Acquisitions are the transactions where organizations either combine or takes control over other to achieve strategic objectives/growth perspective. It provides opportunities to accelerate growth, portfolio diversification or consolidation.

All the aforementioned use cases require the complete historical transactional data of the respective legal entity to ensure uninterrupted business operations and to meet statutory and compliance reporting obligations.

Seamless operation and continuity of connected business applications are critical success factors for any divested or separated entity. Ensuring the continuity of open processes is essential for maintaining reporting dependencies and enabling informed strategic decision-making.

Furthermore, statutory reporting and audit trails of business application data represent critical data assets for any organization. They are fundamental to meeting country-specific data residency requirements and safeguarding the organization against potential legal and compliance risks arising from non-adherence to regulatory obligations.

To perform the separation of business entities/units (Company Code) from an existing SAP ERP landscape (ECC or S4HANA), SAP-DMLT service supports with SAP Company Code Carve Out solution that is tailored to address the aforementioned use cases with a cleaner strategy and data set that can let the organizations function independently and enables stable operations, financial reporting, auditing with the legal obligations.

Typically, objects in scope would be Company Codes and its relevant upper and lower hierarchical organizational elements (such as Controlling Area, Plant, Sales Org, associated master data – Customers, Vendors & Materials etc.)

Technical approach of Company Code Carve out

1. Company Code Clone & Delete

In this approach, the entire source system should be copied technically (clone) including repository objects, customizations, users and the business application data. Later, based on the user provided/confirmed company codes will be retained and all other company codes along with relevant org. units will be deleted from the cloned environment using SAP DMLT toolset.

This will technically delete the out-of-scope Company Codes along with its associated & shared data from target/cloned system, enables faster, cleaner and minimum disruption to business.

Business Outcomes (Value)

  1. Faster, Cleaner approach for Company Code Carve-out
  2. Reduced risk due to functional completeness
  3. Standard SAP tool to perform the carve out activity
  4. Limited testing required compared to general green field approach of only open balances
  5. Business Process Continuity & Faster realization
  6. Data history for statutory reporting needs

Transformation Scenario Limitations

  1. Suitable for smaller database
  2. Any additional data transformation expectation can't be embedded together

2. Company Code Transfer/Migration

As the name suggests, in this approach we will transfer / migrate only the relevant Company Codes with associated org. units along with the respective data sets only to the target empty system. It avoids the need for full copy of the source system compared to earlier explained 'Clone & Delete' approach.

P.S: Shell creation is the process of building a new & empty SAP system with complete repository, cross client objects and client specific customizing of source system without master & transaction data

Business Outcomes (Value)

  1. Minimal data footprint
  2. Lower infrastructure costs/repurpose any existing landscape
  3. Better control on the data set & objects (selection of relevant application) to be moved to target system/environment
  4. Possible to accommodate qualified transformations in any data harmonization (such as Rename of org objects, master data)
  5. Best suited for scenarios with a limited number of company codes being divested or for any country-specific carve outs - where the data set to be separated represents only a small or minimal portion of the overall database volume

Transformation Scenario Limitations

  1. Would require a longer project duration compared to clone-and-delete approach, as each application must be handled individually and might require additional, specific transformation capabilities embedded in the transfer logic.
  2. Relatively higher effort investment on validations and reconciliations

Realization Methodology of Company Code Carve-Out

Once a company code is productive or live with transactional data, any changes become restrictive. Moving configuration changes without transforming the already posted documents can lead to data inconsistencies in the live system, potentially causing critical disruptions in open business processes.

To separate any Company Code(s) from the live system, both the configuration, master and it’s associated transactional data must be handled consistently. For deletion of existing posted documents or transforming open documents, the SAP DMLT Company Code Carve Out Service is required. For more information, refer to the Company Code Carve Out Solution Brief.

Such data transformations in a live production system demand a highly precise and controlled changeover, ensuring consistency at the database level and seamless continuity of SAP software operations. Therefore, every relevant business scenario should be carefully reviewed in the system, considering current configurations and customizations across all connected application modules.

SAP DMLT offers a technical feasibility study to outline system data dependencies and prerequisite conditions. This is followed by a detailed customer workshop with the obtained statistics from the system. The realization of the transformation requirement is delivered under a separate professional consulting contract and executed as a project.

The overall delivery process is broadly divided into two parts:

1. Pre-Qualification of Requirement

It is essential to pre-qualify the business requirement to ensure the right-fit transformation solution is offered. This starts with gathering preliminary details about the business requirement and application usage within the system through a technical questionnaire, available at Mergers and Acquisitions, Divestments, Restructuring.

Based on the received responses, SAP can propose a feasibility study to assess the system, evaluate overall complexity, and deliver a detailed proposal. This assessment typically takes around two weeks, including the deployment of analysis tools in the system.

An SAP Solution Architect/Expert will perform the system assessment and present the key insights during a customer workshop session. This is followed by the provision of commercial details through the designated local SAP Market Unit account representatives.

2. Project Delivery

Depending on the complexity of the engagement, the required number of mock execution cycles will be determined during the scoping phase. In general, data transformation projects of this nature involve a minimum of 2-3 mock cycles before the production data cutover. The purpose of a mock execution is to simulate the complete production data carve-out/deletion, validate the results, and estimate the overall downtime required for the production system.

Each mock execution cycle includes several phases, which can be broadly classified as follows:

2.1 Technical Preparation Phase of Add-On (Uptime):

These types of transformation capabilities are deployed into the customer system as a separate add-on, delivered as a transportable content. The mock execution system should be a recent copy of the production system, with identical or closely matching hardware configuration that of the production environment. Once the tool content is deployed, the technical preparation phase begins, during which SAP DMLT experts prepare the transformation tool. This phase typically takes 2–3 weeks and includes uptime activities and the generation of technical programs for the relevant table-fields.

2.2 Data Carve-Out Phase (Downtime):

After the uptime preparatory activities are completed, the agreed business downtime begins. During this period, Company Code Carve-Out is performed directly at the database table-field level. All relevant Company Codes are separated along with the relevant Org Units in the respective application areas (e.g., FI-GL, FI-AA, ML, CO etc).

2.3 Post-Processing Testing Phase (Uptime):

Once the data carve-out phase is completed, the transformed system is handed over to the customer’s IT/application team for a sanity check during downtime. Upon successful completion of the sanity check, the transformed system is reopened for normal business operations.

Engagement with relevant SAP team or SAP Experts

If your business requirement involves any transformation related to Company Code aspects in a live, productive SAP ECC or S/4HANA system, you can engage with the SAP Data Management and Landscape Transformation (DMLT) consulting team through following ways;

  1. Create a Support Incident
    Submit an incident via the SAP ONE Support Launchpad under the component: CA-LT-SRV (Landscape Transformation Services), Or
  2. Contact via Email
    Reach out directly to the DMLT Global Consulting Experts Email: sap_dmlt_gce@sap.com

The SAP DMLT team will guide you through the pre-assessment process, feasibility evaluation, and project-based service engagement tailored to your specific system and transformation needs.

Reference SAP OSS Notes:

481938 - System Landscape Optimization Services

1462004 - SAP LT: Transformation Solution 'Company Carve-out'

Disclaimer: This blog is intended only for knowledge sharing about Company Code Carve-out/Delete service on SAP ERP (ECC or S/4 HANA) system which might help customer in their landscape transformation requirements. It should not be considered as guideline for performing such transformation services on their own in any of the SAP landscape. Any data transformation efforts should be thoroughly assessed in consultation with qualified subject matter experts, supported by a comprehensive system study, and aligned with the appropriate approvals from your auditors and relevant stakeholders.

entry.title: SAP Concur Payroll Integration via ICS
entry.content:
The Payroll Integration allows clients to transfer payroll data to their SAP Payroll system as a method for reimbursing their employees for approved business expenses.

SAP Concur ICS (Integration with Concur Solutions) enables seamless, automated data flow between SAP Concur Expense and SAP ERP systems like SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC.

ICS supports payroll integration by enabling expense reimbursement data to flow directly from Concur to SAP payroll systems.

Payroll Integration helps to reduce manual work by automatically transferring expense financial posting data to the SAP Payroll system.

Once an expense report is successfully posted in SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC system, it will automatically send the report to SAP Payroll system for processing, allowing companies to reimburse out of pocket expenses and accurately assign wage types and tax related details. ‑of‑pocket expenses and accurately assign wage types and tax‑related details.

A payroll document is created only after its related expense report is fully posted, ensuring accuracy and consistency in payroll and financial data.

Systems Involved:

  • SAP Concur – where employees create and submit expense reports
  • SAP FI – validates and posts the expense (no payment yet)
  • SAP HCM / EC Payroll – stores expense data as HR master data
  • SAP Payroll– calculates tax and pays the employee via payroll

System Landscape Options:

Payroll integration via ICS works with:

  • SAP FI + SAP HCM in the same system
  • SAP FI and HCM in separate systems
  • SAP FI in cloud + HCM on‑premise
  • EC Payroll (SuccessFactors)

1. Configure Payroll Integration in SAP Concur

Step 1: Login to Concur and navigate to Administration → Expense → Group Configuration

Select the respective group and Tick (✓) Enable Financial Integration setting and then Tick (✓) Enable Payroll Integration setting.

Once these settings are enabled, concur admin can be able to see the “Payroll Integration” setting under Company → Tools → Payroll Integration

Step 2: Under “Payroll Settings” select the expense group for which payroll Integration is activated. Tick (✓) the check box to use the payroll integration to reimburse employees belong to this group. Once this setting is enabled, all expenses will be paid through payroll.

Enter the below clearing External wage type codes that are maintained in HR/HCM system

  • Cash Advance Clearing Wage Type: This code is used to deduct the cash advances from the employee’s payable amount.
  • Personal Expense Clearing Wage Type: This code is used to deduct the employee’s pay by the amount they spent for personal use on the company card.
  • Payroll Clearing Account Code: This code is used to clear the payroll liability after the amount has been paid to the employee.
  • Benefits in Kind Wage Type: This code is used to record amounts that are treated as a taxable benefit for the employee.

Fill in the Default Wage Types fields. These wage types will be applied when no expense‑specific wage type has been defined on the Wage Types tab.

Once all the required details are entered, click on “Save” button to save the payroll settings.

Step 3: Navigate to “Wage Types” tab and map the wage type codes to the expenses maintained in SAP HR/HCM. This overwrites the default wage type mapping maintained in the Payroll Settings “Default Wage Types” settings.

Once all the wage types are mapped to the expenses, click on “Save” button to save the wage type mappings.

Note: If “Enable Payroll Reimbursement” checkbox is enabled then all the expenses will be transferred to SAP Payroll even if “Transfer Amounts to Payroll?” flag is not selected in the “Wage Types” tab.

To use Payroll for reporting only, then deactivate "Enable Payroll Reimbursement" setting under “Payroll Settings” tab.

If this setting is disabled, then we need to select the expense types that should be transferred to payroll only for reporting/taxation.

Navigate to “Wage Types” tab and select the respective expense types that should be sent only for reporting/taxation. Tick (✓) "Transfer Amounts to Payroll" Check Box for selected expense types. Click on “Save” to save the Wage Types settings. These expenses will be reimbursed via Accounts Payable but not via Payroll.

Note: Taxable travel allowances always flow to payroll.

Step 4: Configure Payroll Wizard in SAP Concur ICS

  • Login to SAP ECC or S/4 HANA system and execute the T-Code: “CTE_SETUP”
  • Open “Integration Activities” and enable “Payroll” connection
  • Configure “Payroll” ICS wizard with required mappings according to client requirements for payroll posting

 

2. Expense Report Payroll Process

  High Level Architecture Diagram:

Process Flow: SAP Concur → SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA (FI & Payroll Postings) → SAP Concur → SAP HCM → Payroll Run → Employee paid via payroll

Step 2.1: SAP Concur – Expense Report Process (Top Blue Box from above process diagram)

  • Employee creates and submits an expense report in SAP Concur (E.g., Breakfast, Taxi, hotel)
  • Review & fully approve the expense report by respective approvers & Processors.
  • SAP Concur sends all the fully approved expense reports which are in payment status “Processing Payment” to SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA (This is mandatory, even for payroll reimbursement)

Step 2.2: SAP Concur Expense and Payroll Posting in SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA via ICS (Yellow Box from above process diagram)

  • SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA automatically imports Expense reports into ICS wizard under Financial Postings → Concur Documents based on the scheduled import job.
  • SAP ICS validates the imported reports and verifies the associated cost objects, G/L accounts, and company codes.
  • Once all the expense data is processed without any errors, then expense documents are posted to SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA (on Payroll Clearing Account) successfully.
  • For Out-of-Pocket‑of‑Pocket expenses, SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA doesn’t issue a direct payment to the employee or vendor. Instead, it posts the amount to a Payroll Clearing GL account which is maintained in “Payroll Settings” in SAP Concur, which allows the payroll team to reimburse the employee through their regular salary process.
  • SAP ICS sends success or failure status back to Concur
    • If Expense posting is failed → process ends here and employee/processor needs to correct and resubmit expense report in Concur
    • If Expense Posting is successful → Payroll process will continue

Important rule: No FI posting = No Payroll Posting

  • Once the FI posting is successful, SAP ECC or SAP S/4 HANA automatically imports successfully posted expense reports into Payroll ICS wizard for payroll posting based on the scheduled import job.

Step 2.3: Transfer of expense data to HCM via Concur

  • Once FI and Payroll posting is successful, Concur automatically triggers the HCM and sends the expense data from Concur to SAP HCM
  • Below details are transferred to HCM:
  • Employee ID
  • Amount
  • Expense types
  • Mapped wage types

Step 2.4: SAP HCM Master Data Setup for Concur Payroll Postings (Green Box from above process diagram)

 Import Reports

  • SAP HCM receives payroll-relevant expense data

Create Info Type 0579

  • Data is written into Info Type 0579
  • Info Type 0579 = External Wage Type Components

Each expense line item becomes:

  • A wage type
  • With an amount
  • Assigned to the employee
  • Stored temporarily for payroll

Key point:  Info Type 0579 is a staging area, but no employee payment yet 

Step 2.5:  Payroll Process

 Execute Payroll

Payroll program run for the payroll period and calculates the amount to be reimbursed for the employee after the necessary deductions. Employees can be able to see net reimbursement and deductions in their salary pay slip.

Note: Payment is made via Standard payroll bank transfer

Step 2.6: Concur Audit Trail update

Once the payroll posting is successful, then the status of the report will be automatically updated in Audit trail in Concur.

To view the Audit trail, select the respective report and go to → Details → Audit Trail to track the status in the expense report. 

3. Monitor Concur Payroll Posting Document Failures

Login to Concur and navigate to Company→ Tools → Payroll Integration → Posting failures tab. Under this tab, concur admin can be able to see all the failure posting documents.

Concur Admins can search for failed postings using the Report ID, Report Name, Employee ID, First Name, or Last Name.

By selecting a checkbox next to an expense report, admin can retry the payroll transfer by clicking on “Retry Payroll Transfer button” at the bottom of the screen.

4. Monitor Concur Payroll Document status

Under “Document Status” tab, admin can be able to search and check the status of the expense reports that are in “Success/Acknowledged/Ready”

5. General FAQ’s

Q1: What types of expenses are sent to Payroll?

A: Only out-of-pocket (cash) expenses—those paid personally by the employee—are sent to payroll for reimbursement. Corporate card transactions typically do not post through payroll unless any expense types selected for tax/reporting purpose. ‑of‑pocket (cash) expenses—those paid personally by the employee—are sent to payroll for reimbursement. Corporate card transactions typically do not

Q2: How are personal expenses handled by Payroll?

A: Any personal transactions charged to a company paid card are recorded under the Personal Expense Clearing wage type and deducted from the employee’s total payroll reimbursement. 

Q3: What are the key limitations associated with Payroll Integration?

A:

1) Partial employee payments in FI and HR are not supported and require customer‑specific enhancements, such as implementing a BADI in ECC or SAP S/4HANA On‑Premise.

2) Cash advance cannot be supported through Payroll Integration, as the feature does not support paying or posting cash advances

3) Payroll Integration will not support payments for IBCP, CBCP, or other company‑paid card transactions, since these are not intended to flow through payroll.

entry.title: Close Offboarding Processes automatically using Integration Center
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post, we will walk through how to automatically close offboarding processes in SAP SuccessFactors using Integration Center. Specifically, we will configure a scheduled integration that updates processes based on the employee’s termination date. By the end of this guide, you will understand how to set up the integration, map the required fields, and ensure the process status is correctly updated.

This configuration is useful for us, in the sense that often required tasks are not all completed on the platform and therefore the job responsible for closing the processes does not work, requiring manual cancellation.

1. Choose the Integration Type

The first step is to choose the integration type. In this case, we will use the Scheduled Trigger type and select SuccessFactors as both the source and destination system.

2. Select the Start Entity

For the start entity, search for ONB2Process.

3. Map the Process ID and the default values

In my case, I set the default values for Process Status and Task Status to "Completed". However, this configuration also works with other status values, depending on your requirement.

Important note: The operation must be set to Update/Merge.

Next, we need to map the Process ID. To do this, click on "Set as associated field" and search for Process ID from Process.

4. Define the Logic

In our scenario, we needed to close offboarding processes when the termination date was 30 days in the past, so we used the target date from process that corresponds to termination date :

5. Schedule and Test

After completing the configuration, you only need to schedule the job and test it. 🙂

The expected result is:

  • Process Status: Completed

  • Task Status: Closed

 

Optional: Cancelling Instead of Completing

If instead of completing the process you want to cancel it, you must also map the Offboarding Cancellation Reason field:

Conclusion

In this post, we demonstrated how to configure a scheduled integration in SAP SuccessFactors to automatically complete or cancel offboarding processes. By selecting the correct integration type, mapping the required fields, and applying appropriate date logic, you can fully automate offboarding closure when the required tasks have not been completed. This approach helps ensure data accuracy, reduces manual effort, and keeps your offboarding workflows aligned with business requirements.

entry.title: Re: Sユーザが削除できない
entry.content:
弊社バックエンドにて調査したところ、S-user * は「Partner users」ロールを保有しております。 このロールを他のS-userに移行しない限り、お客様ご自身で該当S-userを削除することはできません。

先ほど弊社にて該当S-user * を削除いたしましたが、 今後の利用上の不便を防ぐため、可能な限り「Partner users」ロールを他のS-userに移行されることをお勧めいたします。

なお、このロール移行作業は「Partner Support Team」のみが実施可能ですので、 以下のKBAをご参照の上、同チームまでご連絡いただけますようお願い申し上げます。

KBA 1271517 - Important Contact descriptions and how to view/change S-users with important functions - SAP for Me

関連する内容を抜粋致します、ご確認するようにお願い申し上げます。

To add/remove partner roles and authorizations for S-user IDs, the company's Partner Security Manager (S-user) must make the changes in the SAP Partner Portal according to KBA 2609123. Or, contact partner support using the partner Get Help page or contact SAP Partner Support using the "Get Support" on the partner "Get assistance" dashboard.

Tip: If you're not sure who your Partner Security Manager (PRM_SMGR) is or you need to update this user, you must contact partner support using the "Get Support" on the partner "Get assistance" dashboard.

entry.title: Automating Probation Period Extension Using Intelligent Services Center in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Automatic Probation Extension using Intelligent Service Center

Intelligent Service Center (ISC) is a tool provided by SAP in Employee Central that helps recognize events such as hiring, recruiting, time-off requests, and changes in job information like location or job title and etc. There is a standard set of events available in ISC. In this blog, we’ll walk through a requirement that helped automate a manual HR task with the help of ISC & IC.

Business Requirement:
If an employee who is under probation applies for sick leave, their probation period must be extended by the number of leave days taken.

Why this is needed:
Currently, HR has to manually check whether the employee who submitted the leave request is still under probation. If the employee is under probation, HR then needs to manually calculate the new probation end date based on the number of sick leave days taken.

There are chances that HR may overlook whether the employee is under probation, forget to update the probation period, or make errors while calculating the extension date.

To avoid this manual effort and reduce the risk of errors, ISC along with IC has been used to automate this entire process.

Solution Overview:

Our solution is a combination of:

  • Configurations in Time off object
  • ISC – preferred because this change takes place immediately instead of us waiting for the job to run.
  • IC – to change the extended date in job information

Configuration in Time off Object:

A field was created to store the extended probation end date.

This field gets populated during the creation of leave request.

This field ensured the logic is applied to only probation users.

Event Detection using ISC:

ISC by standard can detect Time off request.

In ISC we have configured only to detect the time off request in case a probation user applies sick leave.

So basically 2 condition:

  • Checks if the user is under probation
  • Checks if the leave type selected is Sick leave

Triggering Integration for Probation Update

After all conditions were met:

ISC triggered an Integration Center job

The following data was passed from Time off object to Job Information:

  • User ID
  • Event date (as absence start date)
  • Event reason (set the value as: Probation Extension)
  • Updated probation end date

Important Note:

Make sure to add save rule in Time off object to invoke the ISC - trigger worker absence event.

Configuration Flow:

Sick Leave Applied → Save Rule Calculates Extension → Leave Approved → ISC Event Triggered → Integration Updates Probation End Date

Conclusion:

As a result, HR users no longer need to manually track employees who apply for leave or update probation extension details themselves. By combining ISC, IC, and business rules, different levels of automation can be achieved in SuccessFactors. This not only reduces manual effort but also helps streamline HR processes and improve efficiency going forward.

entry.title: Re: Understanding SAP HANA High Availability - Technical Scope, Mechanisms, and Design Boundaries
entry.content:
Hi @akuller,

good point.

The article mainly focuses on the takeover-oriented HA case.
In Active/Active (read-enabled secondary), the secondary is not a second transactional system. The primary remains the only write system, while the secondary can serve read-only queries.

The access happens via SQL statement routing: the client asks the nameserver for a suitable target and may be directed to the secondary for supported read operations.
If the client cannot validate the proposed target against its known topology, it falls back to the existing connection, this is where the routing warnings typically come from.

So SR still provides availability, and Active/Active only adds optional read workload offloading.

entry.title: Re: How to calculate measures on property level and copy it to lowest dimension level
entry.content:
@SDMS90 

You can do this using Advance Formulas in SAC Data Actions. You would need to use a FOREACH using property to process the product members with identical product groups. Below is a rough structure you can use but you would need to adjust as per your dimensions and measures of the model.

FLOAT @sumsales
FLOAT @sumquantity
FLOAT @avgprice
FOREACH [d/product].[p/productgroup]

@sumsales = RESULTLOOKUP([d/measure] = "sales")
@sumquantity = RESULTLOOKUP([d/measure] = "quantity")
@avgprice = @sumsales/@sumquantity
DATA([d/measure] = "avgprice")=@avgprice

ENDFOR

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: RITA - Tax Ctry/Reg in standard reports
entry.content:
Good day @sanne_larsen 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you are using the correct method. The easiest way to isolate VAT by tax registration in a RITA setup, is typically through the statutory tax reporting flow (for example Advance Return) and filtering by Tax Country. That said, you are not limited to only that one report.

Question: are we missing anything? Is there any other way report we can extract? 

Answer: RITA is positioned to be used together with statutory tax reports, which you execute via Run Statutory Reports (for example VAT Return, EC Sales List, etc.). So, that confirms that your approach is correct, but you should also consider running the relevant report variants from Run Statutory Reports, not only the classic Advance Return app.

You can run additional standard options to analyse your VAT posting with:

  1. Run Statutory Reports: this allows you to carry out country specific (where RITA is supported) reporting.
  2. Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting: Allows tax item analysis at reporting level, period assignment, etc.
  3. Display Journal Entries: serves you as a finance audit view for the underlying postings.

See the following references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/918b3775893645e2943d29780cc4b7c0.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4de200b26d5d42bbacae69c6bf6b9923/0a50b7fec5594e738568b1808f02625e.html?locale=en-US

Regarding your request to add Tax Country or Region in Display Line Items in GL, note that the Public Cloud does not expose every standard field for you to select in every standard analytical app. So, what you are seeing is normal. An example is documented in KBA 3379915. So, your condition is basically right:

  • You can, where allowed, add custom extension fields to some standard apps.
  • But where there is no standard field exposure in that app’s query, you cannot always turn it on with key user settings.

Here is a quick guide in your system:

Open Run Statutory Reports to validate RITA relevant reporting entry point. Select the VAT report for NL and your BE foreign registration setup, filter by Tax Country to isolate one registration number postings.

In Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting, filter by your company code, tax country, reporting period, etc. Use it to review tax items and how they are assigned to reporting periods. See,  VAT Report and VAT Declaration for more details.

In Display Journal Entries, you can filter available fields and drill down to line item level to verify tax posting.

If you must have Tax Country as a reporting dimension in a line item app, the Public Cloud supports you using an app or query that already exposes the tax dimensions you need, or you build a custom analytical report based on a released data source that includes the tax country dimension, instead of you trying to force it into F2217.

Here are some references you may also consider:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/820a2baaa1f848a8bcc39b5a75feab58.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/533228e1e854433ab16d013f161ca509.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/3166104d074642d0953b43fe35fd488a.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Employee Confidential Profile in SAP SuccessFactors Built Using Story Reports
entry.content:
In today’s data-driven talent landscape, succession planning is no longer just about identifying successors — it’s about making informed, strategic decisions based on comprehensive employee insights.

One of the most powerful ways to enable this is by creating an Employee Confidential Profile using Story Reports in SAP SuccessFactors. This report consolidates critical talent data from multiple modules into a single, secure, executive-ready view designed specifically for succession discussions.

Overview

The Employee Confidential Talent Profile is a centralized Story Report built for Succession Planning that pulls data from multiple SuccessFactors sources, including:

  • Employee Photo
  • Employee Talent Profile
  • Performance & Potential Ratings
  • Assessment Data
  • Learning History
  • Development Goals
  • Talent Pool Nomination
  • Succession Nomination
  • Nominated Successors
  • Competency Data including self and manager rating

This report is typically used by:

  • HR Business Partners
  • Talent Management Teams
  • Leadership during Talent Review & Calibration sessions

It provides a 360-degree view of an employee’s readiness, capability, and growth trajectory — all in one structured and confidential format.

Key Features

Consolidated Talent Overview

The report combines multiple datasets into one profile view:

  • Current Role & Department
  • Tenure & Career History
  • Key Competencies
  • Risk of Loss
  • Impact of Loss

This eliminates the need to navigate across multiple modules.

Performance & Potential Trend Analysis

  • Performance and Potential ratings over time
  • Trend indicators (improving, stable, declining)

This allows leaders to analyze growth consistency and leadership trajectory.

Assessment & Readiness Data

  • Readiness levels (R1, R2, R3)
  • Successor nomination status
  • Assessment results
  • Talent flags (High Potential, Future Leader, etc.)

This is critical during succession slate discussions.

Learning & Development Insights

  • Completed learning courses
  • Certifications
  • Ongoing training programs
  • Individual Development Goals
  • Development Goal progress %

This helps evaluate whether the employee is actively closing competency gaps.

Employee Picture & Confidential Layout

Including the employee picture enhances:

  • Visual recognition during talent reviews
  • Engagement in calibration discussions

Since it is a confidential report, it includes:

  • Role-based permissions
  • Restricted access
  • No export rights (if required)

Benefits

Single Source of Truth

Brings together fragmented talent data into one powerful profile.

Better Succession Decisions

Leaders can evaluate readiness based on historical trends, not just current ratings.

Time Efficiency

Reduces manual preparation of talent review decks.

Improved Talent Transparency

Supports objective discussions backed by data.

Configuration Approach

Below is a structured approach to configure the Employee Confidential Profile using Story Reports:

Step 1: Enable Required Domains in Story Reporting

Ensure access to relevant schemas:

  • Employee Profile
  • Performance Management
  • Succession
  • Learning
  • Goal Management
  • Development Planning

These domains must be enabled in Manage Data → Report Schemas.

Step 2: Create a Cross-Domain Story

  1. Go to Reporting → New → Story
  2. Select Domain
  3. Add required domains:
    • Job Information
    • Performance Management
    • Succession Planning
    • Learning
    • Goal Management
    • Development Planning

Establish joins using:

  • User ID
  • Person ID External

Step 3: Build Report Sections

Design the profile in structured sections:

Section A – Employee Snapshot

  • Name
  • Job Title
  • Department
  • Manager
  • Photo
  • Tenure

Section B – Performance & Potential History

  • Performance rating (last 3 years)
  • Potential rating

Section C – Succession & Readiness

  • Critical Position indicator
  • Successor nominations
  • Readiness level (R1/R2/R3)
  • Risk of Loss

Section D – Learning & Development

  • Completed courses
  • Certifications
  • Development goals
  • Goal completion %

Step 4: Apply Confidentiality Controls

  • Restrict via Role-Based Permissions (RBP)
  • Control target population
  • Restrict export if required
  • Limit drill-down capability

Typically, access is granted only to:

  • HRBPs
  • Talent Managers
  • Senior Leadership

Step 5: Add Dynamic Filters

Enable filtering by:

  • Department
  • Business Unit
  • Location
  • Talent Pool
  • Critical Position flag

This allows live talent review meetings without multiple versions of the report.

Best Practices

  • Keep the design executive-friendly (avoid too many charts).
  • Use conditional formatting for readiness & performance ratings.
  • Highlight critical position successors separately.
  • Include performance trend rather than single-year rating.
  • Test data joins carefully to avoid duplication from multiple modules.

Final Thoughts

The Employee Confidential Profile built using Story Reports transforms succession planning from a manual, slide-based process into a real-time, analytics-driven experience.

By integrating data from:

  • Talent Profile
  • Performance & Potential History
  • Assessment Data
  • Learning History
  • Development Goals

Organizations can move toward evidence-based succession decisions rather than subjective evaluations.

In a competitive talent market, having the right successor ready at the right time is not optional — it’s strategic.

If you’re working on Succession Planning enhancements in SuccessFactors, this is one of the most impactful reports you can implement.

entry.title: Re: Change of declarant for VAT reporting
entry.content:
Good day @PlamenTraychev 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, the declarant data used in Run Statutory Reports is maintained in the statutory reporting configuration activity SSCUI 101071 under the Reporting Entity and the report activity properties.

Here are links to guide you: KBA 3287422, Setting Up the VAT Declaration - Monthly Report, Setting Up the Report and KBA 3453645

Note that 1010171 SSCUI ID is scope dependent. You might not be able to see and do the configure for setting up your statutory reporting in CBC if scope item 1J2 is missing in your tenant. You will need to open a case for item XX-S4C-OPR-SRV for that item to be activated.

Let me know if you still need a step by step guide.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Header Billing Plan net value is 0
entry.content:
Mass Maintenance of Billing Plans: Instead of setting up 20+ items manually, you can use Mass Change tools in the GUI (transaction VBOF or via “Change Sales Orders” with multiple selection). Custom Fiori extensions or Custom CDS queries to update billing plans in bulk.

Copying Billing Plans: Create one item with the correct billing plan, then copy it across items using GUI functions or custom enhancements.

SAP Roadmap Check: SAP has been gradually enhancing Fiori apps. If header-level billing plan support is critical, it’s worth checking the latest release notes or raising an influence request with SAP.

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
Thanks as always @Marian_Zeis for your thoughts; it is perfectly fine to disagree - like I said in my post, we are all adults here, and disagreements are often how we learn and grow too. I understand your frustration and giving up the fight is not an unusual path to take, I get it, and the community is bigger than just the content on any given platform - for example, publishing on one's own blog (like you and I do) is a great way to grow oneself and find one's own voice, and grow the community as a whole too.

Beyond encouraging folks to acknowledge good posts, my primary concern is doing something about the avalanche of moderator reports that I think are (a) inappropriate and (b) causing such a load on @AnnePetteroe & the team which means they have no time to do anything else.

Even if some of the community start doing this, it may make a difference. Turning the giant cargo ship is no mean feat, and will take a while. But I think it's worth a try.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Document Adjustment - most common mistakes and questions
entry.content:
To add to this blog: if you're using Version Management for your Purchase Orders, which in turn triggers the release strategy (in our case Flexible Workflow), Automatic Document Adjustment (ADA) is creating but not completing the newest version. This would then still be a manual task after running ADA. 

The most straight forward approach seems to be to execute this automatic document adjustment job with a next job step along, which should read the spool (PO result list) of the previous job step, then via a BAPI or other solution to complete the versions of those POs. But it's still a hassle.

Building a SaaS solution is exciting.  Building one that scales cleanly across customers without breaking the core?  That’s where things get interesting.

In our upcoming webinar, “Building multitenant SaaS solutions with SAP Build: A partner's guide to innovate for scale,” we’ll walk through how you can design, deploy, and commercialize multitenant applications on SAP Business Technology Platform while staying true to clean core principles with SAP Build.

We’ll cover the real journey, from architecture and ERP-agnostic design to pricing models, provisioning, and cost structures for dev, test, demo, and production.  Plus, we’ll connect the dots between technical decisions and commercial impact.

Honestly, multitenancy isn’t just a technical choice.  It’s a business multiplier.  One code line.  Multiple customers.  Controlled extensions.  Stronger margins.

Whether you’re in the SAP PartnerEdge Open Ecosystem or a Build partner refining your portfolio, this session is designed to give you clarity and confidence.

Register now and start building for scale, the smart way.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013076.html

entry.title: Re: Triggering SAP Datasphere/BDC Task Chains from SAP BTP ABAP Cloud using the Task Chain REST API
entry.content:
Hi Niroshini,

yes indeed. It's possible.

You typically deal with the following classes in your implementation and you've created an SM59 HTTP connection for the API endpoint.

Important ABAP classes:

CL_HTTP_ENTITYHandle request/response body and headers
/UI2/CL_JSONJSON serialization/deserialization
CL_ABAP_CODEPAGECharacter encoding conversions
CL_HTTP_UTILITYURL encoding and utility functions
CL_HTTP_CLIENTHTTP Client/Request handling

Best regards, Stefan

entry.title: Re: Designing a Multi‑Tenant Solutions on SAP BTP – Architecture and Prototype Walkthrough
entry.content: Nice blog. But you skipped the tenant isolation in the database, which is not really ensured with the service manager and a service-instance schema. This schema cannot be managed by the database independently from other schemas. That is a real gap in your solution. The right way is using the HANA Cloud native multitenancy capability, with customer-specific encrypted database tenants (containing schemas or HDI containers), resource control and independent lifecycle.   For native database tenants you use the Tenant Management Service instead of the Service Manager. See here for more details: https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-multitenancy/sap-hana-cloud-multitenancy?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: How to make a field mandatory or warning trigger for...
entry.content:
Dear @jerrysam,

Option A: Recommended

Use Cloud Badi MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD (Check Invoice Data) to raise a W (warning) or E (error) message when the header KSeF field is empty. This is the same mechanism SAP recommends to enforce supplier-invoice checks in Public Cloud

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3673139

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/5a27c5f57c3f4dffa6881ca587b22571.html

Steps to do:

Option A:

1. Make sure the field exists on the invoice header:

  • If you already have a standard KSeF reference number/KSeF field available for supplier invoices use it SAP provides configuration/support for maintaining KSeF reference number in supplier invoices
  • If you don’t have it, create a custom field in Custom Fields and Logic with business context publish it and add it to the UI key user extensibility / Adapt UI. Available fields you can find in Badi below:

 

2. Go to Custom Logic

  • Create a new implementation for Badi: MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD

 

3. Implement the check

  • Use the ACTION parameter to decide when to show/stop:
    • Use action CHECK if you want users to see the message while entering the invoice when they press Check/Simulate
    • Use action POST / SAVE AS COMPLETED if you want to block posting when KSeF is missing
  • If the KSeF field is initial/blank append a message to the provided MESSAGES table:
    • Warning: message type W (allows posting, but warns)
    • Error: message type E (prevents posting)

For example:

    " Example 4: Issue an warning message

    " Only issue the message if YOUR FIELD is not filled

    " This message will not prevent any actions, however it will be displayed to the user

    " Please note that warning, success and information messages will only be displayed during the check action

    IF headerdata-YOURFIELD IS INITIAL AND

       action = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_action_check.

      APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_messagetype_warning

                     messageid = 'MRM_BADI_CLOUD'

                     messagenumber = '001'

                     messagevariable1 = YOUR FIELD is not filled.'  ) TO messages.

 

    ENDIF.

More details you will find inside Badi clicking:

 

4. Activate the implementation and test in Create Supplier Invoice Advanced (MIRO)

Option B:

If SAP already issues a standard message for your scenario and you only want to switch it between I/W/E you can use in CBC ID 102091 Define Message Types for Area M8

 

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2654157

IMPORTANT: You cannot change SAP predefined standard message type entries only customer-specific settings are possible and some changes are restricted by design so in my opinion option A is suitable for your case

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: GL Account not available as Preconditition for Market...
entry.content:
Good day @JavierIbanez 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is not a “forgotten field”. In Manage Solution and Validation Rules, the available precondition fields are restricted based on the business context. Market Segment context derives or validates profitability segment fields and only some drivers are exposed. See Manage Substitution/Validation Rules and KBA 2892205 for more details.

So, it is rather a scope and field exposure limitation of that specific business context in the key user app.

Regarding the availability of GL Account being available in Custom Logic BAdI, this often happens because Runtime structure has more fields and key user rule UI exposes only stable subset. That is based on the clean core key user tools design.

  • If your substitution must be driven by GL account, the clean approach I recommend is to keep the Market Segment rule simple or skip the rule app for this case. Implement the condition and substitution in FINS_MARKET_SEGMENT_SUB. This will help you avoid creating and populating a ZZ_GLACCOUNT like workaround field.
  • Alternatively, if your real need is GL account-based substitution/validation during posting, then check whether your scenario can be solved with a Journal Entry Item type context where GL Account is exposed. You would then substitute fields that are available in that context, not necessarily market segment extension fields.

Regarding raising an incident, if your expectation is for SAP to expose GL Account as a selectable precondition in Market Segment context, then that would rather be an enhancement request and not an incident. KBA 2892205 also addresses this boundary.

Did you also consider  GL Account Hierarchies ?

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: I Asked 50 SAP Professionals What Scares Them About AI. Here's What They Said.
entry.content:
Hi MioYasutake

This is a really good question. And it highlights the most common problem with every human-in-the-loop scenario I have seen so far: I have not seen one of them that is a loop!

They are a human in a serial approval (Yes/No) step after the AI. There is:

  • very little reasoning showing how the AI came to its conclusion (this is a huge problem because mostly the AI doesn't know so it can't say)
  • very little the human can do beyond approve or reject
  • no path for the human to feed their reasoning back to the AI so that the AI learns better the context of its recommendations and therefore to make better recommendations.

In other words, it is not a loop. The term is mostly a comfort blanket for marketing purposes, so people (customers) will trust the decisions made behind boundaries they cannot see through.

It is covering one bad situation by making another one on top of it.

This is not to say that there are no loops - just that I haven't seen any yet.

If we want to have entry-level people in our business who can learn and perform to the standard we aspire to, we have to provide them with the information context to learn and improve. We have to build real actual functioning loops, and not talk about them or mis-label things that are not loops.

Hope that helps to answer your question.

Cheers
Denver

entry.title: Advance and Accelerate your Mobile Workflows with Embedded AI
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced digital landscape, embedded AI is revolutionizing mobile workflows by bringing intelligence directly into applications. This integration enables faster decision-making, smarter actions, and seamless productivity without the need to switch contexts. Here are several ways embedded AI is transforming mobile experiences:

  • Delivering contextual assistance at the moment of need
    Embedded AI offers timely support precisely when users require it, enhancing decision-making processes directly within the app.
  • Automating repetitive tasks to boost efficiency
    By automating routine tasks, AI frees up time and resources, allowing users to focus on more strategic activities.
  • Unlocking organizational knowledge for faster and smarter decisions
    AI leverages existing organizational data to provide insights that enable quicker and well-informed decision-making.
  • Empowering users on-the-go with real-time, intelligent insights
    Mobile users receive dynamic and contextually relevant information, which enhances their ability to act swiftly and effectively.
  • Transforming mobile apps into proactive productivity tools
    With embedded AI, mobile applications become active parts in driving productivity.

 

UX Components and Patterns for Enhanced Clarity, Trust, and Usability

To ensure the smooth integration of AI into mobile workflows, we have designed UX components and patterns that prioritize clarity, trust, and usability. These UI elements are thoughtfully crafted to embed intelligence directly into applications, thereby enriching the user experience.

AI Notice

The AI notice feature transparently communicates when and how AI is being used within an app, building trust with users by keeping them informed. It is applied on components or pages that contain AI-generated fields, appearing interactively beneath each field. Users can tap a hyperlink within the notice to access additional information about the AI technology used to generate the content, including data usage, privacy, and potential risks.

Learn more about the AI notice pattern in our design guidelines.

AI notice examples

 AI Button

The AI button acts as an entry point for initiating AI-driven actions, enabling users to interact with AI seamlessly. It allows users to activate AI-powered features within a user flow, with the AI icon indicating where such features are available. Tapping the "Generate" button starts the content generation process. During this process, the button updates to "Stop Generating," giving users the option to pause the process at any moment. Once the generation is complete or stopped, the button changes to a "Revise" button, allowing users to make adjustments or regenerate content.

Check out AI buttons to get more details.

AI buttons

AI Acknowledgement

AI acknowledgement ensures informed user consent, capturing and recording acceptance of AI functionalities. It delivers crucial information about AI services to users in a standardized manner, focusing on updates, data privacy, and potential risks, thereby establishing clear user expectations and fostering trust. To keep users informed about any AI service updates, contextual hints are recommended on the settings page, allowing users to review changes. Within the settings, users can also choose to opt out of in-app AI features and data collection. Depending on the definition of opting out, users may disable AI data collection while still being able to use AI features.

For more information, refer to AI Acknowledgement.

AI acknowledgement screens

AI Progress Indicators

AI progress indicators manage user expectations by visually displaying the status during AI processing, ensuring users know their request is actively being processed. Once content generation begins, the indicator changes its state to "processing" or "loading," persisting until the task is completed or stopped. The indicator appears one second after the process starts and remains visible for at least 1000ms to prevent flickering, with pulses between animation loops. Offering users the option to stop the AI generation process is recommended for greater control.

Learn more about AI Progress Indicators.

AI progress indicators

AI Writing Assistant

The AI writing assistant supports content creation by providing contextual help right where it’s needed. It efficiently edits text in input fields using prompts displayed in a panel, helping users refine their input and complete writing tasks smoothly. Tapping the "Writing Assistant" button opens the panel above the keyboard, and users can close it by clicking "x" if they haven't yet edited content. After their first edit, navigation changes to "Cancel" and "Done," where "Done" saves changes and "Cancel" offers a confirmation dialog to keep or discard edits. Users can interact with elements outside the panel and are prompted with a dialog if they attempt to switch text fields. Each quick prompt selection updates the text instantly and increases the version number, allowing users to track changes easily.

Check out the AI Writing Assistant design guideline article for mor details.

AI writing assistant

AI User Feedback

The AI user feedback feature gathers user feedback to continually refine and enhance AI quality and functionality. It enables users to provide positive or negative feedback on their experiences with embedded AI, from component-level to page-level interactions. AI feedback can e accessed through automatic pop-ups after AI experiences, in-line thumbs-up or thumbs-down buttons next to components, or through flexible re-entry points like overflow buttons. For positive feedback, users tap the thumbs-up button, triggering a toast message confirming their submission. For negative feedback, users tap the thumbs-down button, with options to provide additional details, requiring a manual tap on "Submit" to finalize. If users reconsider negative feedback, they can change it to positive by tapping the thumbs-up button in the detail view, and the system updates the feedback accordingly.

Find out more about AI User Feedback.

AI user feedback

 

Use Cases - AI Writing Assistant

In the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app, the AI writing assistant is set to serve as a powerful tool for users to efficiently draft goal descriptions. This planned feature will aid users in bypassing the often tedious and error-prone process of composing these descriptions manually. For instance, when a manager needs to set performance goals for their team members, the AI writing assistant can suggest clear and concise descriptions, ensuring alignment with company objectives and language standards. By providing a few keywords or phrases, users will be able to generate well-structured goal descriptions, reducing errors and saving valuable time. This streamlined process promises to enhance productivity while ensuring consistency and clarity in goal-setting across the organization.  

Goal description using the AI writing assistant

 

Embedded AI Resources

For those eager to integrate embedded AI into their mobile workflows, the following resources are now available for Android and iOS: software development kits, comprehensive design guidelines, and Figma UI kits, all crafted to empower you to harness the potential of embedded AI effectively.

Unlock the power of embedded AI and transform your mobile experience today!

Get Started - In-App AI Design Guidelines for SAP Fiori for Android

Get Started - In-App AI Design Guidelines for SAP Fiori for iOS

Figma UI Kit - SAP Fiori for Android

Figma UI Kit - SAP Fiori for iOS

entry.title: Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA: How Three Integrated Capabilities Enable Safe Global Operations
entry.content:
Compliance is no longer just a regulatory necessity, it is a strategic capability.
Across global supply chains, companies must ensure that products are safe, marketable, and transportable at every stage, making product compliance a critical foundation for uninterrupted operations and sustainable growth.
With SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance, companies embed safety, regulatory alignment, and risk mitigation directly into their core business processes.
By uniting Compliance Document Management, Product Marketability, and Dangerous Goods Management in one integrated solution, organizations ensure global readiness, reduce operational risk, and turn compliance into a true business enabler across the entire product lifecycle.

If you would like to learn more about Product Compliance, the blog posts and inbrief videos below explore each of the three main capabilities within SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance in detail, highlighting key functionalities and demonstrating the business value in practice:

Compliance Document Management – Simplifying Global Regulatory Documentation
How SAP S/4HANA automates the creation, management, and distribution of compliance documents such as SDSs and product labels.
- Automated document generation
- Integrated regulatory checks
- Full transparency through analytics

Blog Post & Video:
Simplifying Global Compliance – How SAP S/4HANA Tr... - SAP Community

Product Marketability – Ensuring Global Readiness & Compliance
How companies can evaluate whether their products meet market-specific requirements and remain globally marketable.
- Embedded regulatory content
- Automated compliance checks across the value chain
- Proactive risk management and faster time-to-market

Blog Post & Video:
Rethinking Product Compliance – How SAP S/4HANA He... - SAP Community

Dangerous Goods Management – Safe and Compliant Global Transport
How SAP S/4HANA helps classify, package, and transport hazardous materials safely and efficiently.
- Embedded dangerous goods classifications
- Automated logistics checks preventing non-compliant shipments
- Real-time visibility into transport permissions and restrictions

Blog Post & Video:
Enhancing Safety and Efficiency – How SAP S/4HANA ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: I Asked 50 SAP Professionals What Scares Them About AI. Here's What They Said.
entry.content:
@MioYasutake 

Thanks for this raw and honest take, it's refreshing to see someone call out the real anxieties in the SAP world without sugarcoating. I've been in SAP consulting for about 5 years now, and yeah, that "confidently wrong" AI fear hits home. Last year on a project, we tested an AI tool for config suggestions, and it nailed 80%, but it bombed on compliance checks took us days to clean up. Made me realize governance isn't optional; it's everything.

On the hope side, I'm in the "curious but uncertain" camp from your poll. AI could free us from the grunt work, letting us focus on strategy, but only if we get the data house in order first. What's one small step you've seen work to ease these fears in teams? Would love to hear!

But, I'm getting this error

DBS-070407: |Loader LoadMapper_ceadfe63-ecac-4390-9fec-e5e58fd49640_target1_1TL_OneS_EXCHANGERATES SQL submitted to database <> resulted in error <General error;258 insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found with guid '8378B4CB2A342A41BE3EDE38A5675F38'>. The SQL submitted is <TRUNCATE TABLE "ODS_PRD"."1TL_OneS_EXCHANGERATES" >. Possible causes: (1) Error in the SQL syntax; (2) Database connection is broken; (3) Database related errors such as transaction log is full, etc.; (4) The user defined in the datastore has insufficient privileges to execute the SQL. entry.title: Re: Cost center MIS not available in system; manual Excel...
entry.content:
Hello @S0027059524 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you are correct. A financial Statement Version is a GL account hierarchy used for financial statement presentation. You cannot add a CO dimension like Cost Center as an additional grouping level inside the same FSV structure.

Question: Is there any standard capability in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to enable Cost Center based grouping within an FSV structure?

Answer:

  • Cost center based grouping inside an FSV is not supported as a standard behaviour.
  • FSV like reporting with Cost Center drilldown is only supported when you use Embedded Analytics (Custom Analytical Queries, Manage KPIs and Reports and standard cost center apps), and optionally you may also use SAC for management reporting.

The following references provides some insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/8e040a45e4db4e6abd426bee0a86dc8a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/c00cbf7fe8464663aee830fb6e7eec13.html

A Public Cloud, clean core best practice is to use a GL account hierarchy (your FSV) plus Cost Center as a characteristic in an analytical report.

Alternatively, if the ask is mainly from the P&L department, start with the delivered cost center analytics (Cost Centers Review Booklet) and cost accounting line item app (Display Line Items Cost Accounting), then align the layout to your business needs.

For more details, consult https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/95063eff5ee0467f8cc04b4ff8f8ca01.html

If the business wants an MIS pack that looks like an FSV that has multiple pages, commentary, scheduling and distribution, then the usual best practice is SAC consuming your S4H analytical query, your GL hierarchy and cost center hierarchy. See Custom Analytical Queries and KBA 2710858 for more insights.

In summary,

  • FSV is a GL account hierarchy and does not supporting adding cost center as an additional grouping level inside the FSV itself.
  • In Public Cloud, the best practice for an FSV like MIS with cost center drilldown is Embedded Analytics; where you maintain the GL hierarchy in Manage Global Hierarchies, build a Custom Analytical Query that includes GL account and cost center, and publish the statement style report in Manage KPIs and reports.
  • For executive packs, consume the same query in SAC.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Material Description 2
entry.content:
Hi  Ravi,
About material description data exactly we have 2 description but what is the difference between it.

in table MAKT u can see 2 description and the Purpose of them:

MAKTX is typically used for printing/displaying, while MAKTG is used for search help to avoid issues with inconsistent letter casing

  • MAKTX (Material Description): Stores the text as it is entered, supporting both upper and lower case letters.
  • MAKTG (Material Description in Upper Case): Automatically stores the uppercase version of MAKTX It is primarily used for consistent search/match code functionality.I hope this help uRegards.Osama Selim
